name: Picacho-Peguera Bicycle Trail
name_original: Carril cicloturista Picacho Peguera - Parque Natural Los Acornocales – circular desde Alcalá de los Gazules
description: Challenging 45.1-mile touring cycling route through Los Alcornocales Natural Park, featuring varied terrain and stunning views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules traverse diverse landscapes within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park, Europe's most extensive cork oak forest. The region features a varied terrain of woodlands, Mediterranean rivers, and rocky peaks, including the Sierra del Aljibe. Cyclists can experience rolling hills, olive orchards, and historic "Pueblos Blancos" nestled in the broader Andalusian countryside. This area offers a range of routes from challenging mountain ascents to more moderate paths through natural reserves. Best touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules The most popular touring cycling route is Picacho-Peguera Bicycle Trail, a 45.1 miles (72.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 38 minutes to complete. This route offers an immersive experience through cork oak forests and mountain vistas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Pico del Montero – Puerto de Membrillo loop from Loma de Taramilla, a difficult 35.0 miles (56.3 km) path. This route includes challenging ascents and provides panoramic views of the surrounding natural park. Local touring cyclists also love the Alcalá de los Gazules – Pico del Montero loop from Alcalá de los Gazules, a 57.9 miles (93.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain and extensive natural park areas, often completed in about 7 hours 53 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcalá De Los Gazules is defined by cork oak forests, Mediterranean rivers, and rocky peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Alcalá De Los Gazules are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 350 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá De Los Gazules's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules, offering a variety of experiences through the region's diverse landscapes. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The terrain around Alcalá De Los Gazules is quite varied. You'll encounter extensive cork oak forests, Mediterranean rivers, and rocky peaks within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park. The broader Andalusian countryside features rolling hills, olive orchards, and charming 'Pueblos Blancos' (white villages), providing a mix of challenging ascents and more moderate paths.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, while many routes offer significant challenges, there are also several moderate options available. Currently, there are 9 moderate touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules, providing enjoyable rides without extreme difficulty. These routes often traverse scenic areas like the Barbate Reservoir.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31522420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de los Gazules – Pico del Montero loop from Alcalá de los Gazules&lt;/a&gt;, covering 57.9 miles with significant elevation, offer demanding ascents and panoramic views. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14569481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Montero – Puerto de Membrillo loop from Loma de Taramilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35.0 miles long and includes challenging climbs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through the stunning Los Alcornocales Natural Park, Europe's most extensive cork oak forest. You can also encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Redondo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/528993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbate Reservoir Dam Path&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5644866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Trail in Parque Natural Los Alcornocales&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to experience the park's unique ecosystems up close.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest accessible by bike?
A: Alcalá de los Gazules itself is a declared Historic Site with a preserved fortified castle tower, ancient town walls, and the Church of St. George. Cycling through the region, you'll pass through charming 'Pueblos Blancos' and can explore areas connected to the 'Route of the Bull'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4118830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España in Medina-Sidonia&lt;/a&gt; is another notable historical landmark you might encounter.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976195838/picacho-peguera-bicycle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picacho-Peguera Bicycle Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40369013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montera del Torero – Charco Redondo Reservoir loop from Cerro de Mófeta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly recommended, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural scenery. Autumn also presents the unique opportunity for wild mushroom foraging in the natural parks.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay along the routes?
A: Alcalá de los Gazules and the surrounding 'Pueblos Blancos' offer various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. While specific establishments along every route aren't listed, these villages provide excellent opportunities to refuel and rest during or after your ride. The El Picacho Recreation Area, just 9km from town, also has camping facilities and a mountain lodge.

Q: Are there any opportunities for wildlife spotting while cycling?
A: Cycling through Los Alcornocales Natural Park offers excellent chances for wildlife observation. The park is home to deer, wild boar, otters, and various raptors. Keep an eye out, especially in quieter sections of the routes, for glimpses of the local fauna.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: Parking is generally available in Alcalá de los Gazules itself, particularly near the town center or at designated parking areas. For routes starting outside the town, such as those from Loma de Taramilla or Cerro de Mófeta, you'll typically find parking options at the trailhead or nearby villages. It's advisable to check specific route details for the most convenient starting points.

Q: What makes touring cycling in Alcalá De Los Gazules unique?
A: Touring cycling here is unique due to its immersion in the vast Los Alcornocales Natural Park, Europe's largest cork oak forest, combined with the charm of the Andalusian 'Pueblos Blancos'. The region offers a blend of challenging mountain vistas, serene Mediterranean river valleys, and rich historical culture, all within a pristine natural reserve.
tips: Alcalá is part of the Ruta del Toro - a tourist itinerary in the province of Cádiz that runs through cattle-raising areas where fighting bulls and the retinta autochthonous breed, prized for its meat, are bred.
Good place to rest surrounded by nature and with magnificent views of Mount Picacho

The Port of Membrillo is located on the Picacho Peguera cycle lane, a route that is heavily traveled by cyclists, especially in the mornings and on weekends. To do this 42.5 km route between the recreational area of Monte Picacho and the Peguera stream that passes through Los Alcornocales Natural Park from September 15 to February 15, it is necessary to consult the natural park office to carry out because there are activities of big game in the area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The full circular route is approximately 72.6 km long and typically takes around 5 hours and 38 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit to cycle through Los Alcornocales Natural Park on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, permission is required to cycle this route, particularly between September 15th and February 15th. This is due to big game activities in the area. You must obtain the permit from the natural park office or the "Ventana del Visitante".

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Picacho Peguera cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, with a mix of paved and unpaved sections. Approximately 41% is paved (including asphalt), and about 48% consists of unpaved dirt tracks, which are often in good condition. Some segments, especially on climbs, can be challenging and may require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a great experience year-round, but be aware that permits are required between September 15th and February 15th due to big game activities. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult and requires very good fitness due to its length and significant elevation gain. It is not generally recommended for beginners or families with young children looking for an easy ride. More experienced cyclists might find it moderately challenging.

faqs.question: What natural landmarks and viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the exceptional environment of Los Alcornocales Natural Park, with cork oaks, holm oaks, and panoramic views of the Barbate river and its reservoir, the Bay of Cadiz, and the valley of Patriste. Notable landmarks include the imposing Picacho peak and the Pico Montero, identifiable by its spherical radar. The route also passes through Puerto de Membrillo.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to access the circular route from Alcalá de los Gazules?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Alcalá de los Gazules, which serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. Specific parking areas within the town may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While Los Alcornocales Natural Park generally allows dogs, specific regulations may apply to cycling routes, especially during hunting seasons. It's best to check with the natural park office regarding current rules for dogs on this particular trail, and always keep them on a leash to protect wildlife.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates significant sections of the Picacho Peguera trail (covering about 57% of the route). It also briefly crosses parts of the [CIMA CD03] Gáliz * Alcalá de los Gazules, [CIMA CD10] Pico Montero (EVA-11), Sendero Subida Al Aljibe, Sendero Subida Al Picacho, Sendero Travesía del Aljibe, and Sendero Garganta de Puerto Oscuro.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or public transport options near the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Alcalá de los Gazules, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. For public transport, you would need to research bus services to Alcalá de los Gazules, as direct public transport to the trailhead itself is unlikely.

faqs.question: What is the main highlight of cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The main highlight is the immersive journey through the heart of Los Alcornocales Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. It offers a deep appreciation for the region's biodiversity, stunning Mediterranean forests, and panoramic views, making it a challenging yet rewarding adventure.
